current_fact.fact_text: On 7 March 1814, Napoleon won the Battle of Craonne in northeastern France, tactically defeating a larger Russian and Prussian force at the cost of heavy casualties. It was a sharp, narrow victory in a wider campaign that was already slipping beyond his control, a reminder that even the most brilliant maneuvers can’t always reverse the tide.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description: Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension and shaped by conviction, remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest. From unrest to resistance, the episode traces rupture and legacy.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

current_fact.match_reason: A ferocious, politically charged rock episode mirrors the intensity and mounting inevitability surrounding Napoleon’s late‑war battlefield victories.

queue.fact_text: In 1799, Napoleon Bonaparte’s army captured the port city of Jaffa during his Middle Eastern campaign; after the siege, French troops massacred more than 2,000 surrendering Ottoman‑Albanian soldiers. The episode remains one of the darkest and most debated moments of his career, where military ambition collided with brutal expediency.

episode.title: Evening Flows #01 - Yasmin - Crescendo in the Medina
episode.description: A journey that starts deep in the medina, slow, mysterious, and textured, then grows into a liberating dance across sub-Saharan landscapes. Ambient soundscapes, Arabic melodies, Afro-Latin rhythms, and funky highlife grooves.
songs: David Visan & Carlos Campos - Crystal Heart
Evan Mars - Ya Zareef Al Tool
Dele Sosimi & Medlar - You No Fit Touch Am (Medlar Remix)

queue.match_reason: The Middle Eastern setting and Arabic melodic textures in the episode resonate with the coastal medina of Jaffa and Napoleon’s Levantine campaign.

queue.fact_text: German U‑boat commander Günther Prien and his submarine U‑47 vanished without a trace on 7 March 1941, after having become legendary for sinking the British battleship HMS Royal Oak inside Scapa Flow. Their disappearance turned one of the most feared crews of the Atlantic into a ghost story of the deep, with theories ranging from minefields to depth‑charge attacks.

episode.title: Morning Drops #33 - Immerse
episode.description: An analogy between diving and immersing in life itself. From calm suspension to warm, rhythmic flow, the selection moves like water — fluid, organic, and alive.
songs: Pino D'Angiò - Un Po' d'Uva e un Liquore

queue.match_reason: An episode built around immersion and water mirrors the mystery of a submarine and its crew disappearing beneath the Atlantic surface.

queue.fact_text: On 7 March 1951, United Nations forces under General Matthew Ridgway launched Operation Ripper during the Korean War, a massive offensive that used coordinated infantry, artillery, and air power to push Chinese and North Korean troops back toward the 38th parallel. It marked a strategic shift from desperate defense to a methodical attempt at retaking Seoul.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

queue.match_reason: The high‑voltage rock and sense of forward momentum fits a large, coordinated offensive designed to shock enemy lines back across Korea.

queue.fact_text: That same day in 1951, Iranian prime minister Ali Razmara was assassinated outside a Tehran mosque by Khalil Tahmasebi, a member of the radical group Fada'iyan‑e Islam. The killing accelerated a political crisis over oil nationalization and helped set the stage for the seismic power struggles that would reshape Iran in the coming decades.

episode.title: Morning Drops #44 - Residente
episode.description: Yasmin traces the life of René Pérez Joglar (Residente) as a timeline of resistance, lyrical obsession, and identity. From political critique to fragile confession, the episode explores how one voice can disrupt power.

queue.match_reason: A politically charged episode about challenging power systems reflects the radical motivations and cascading consequences of Razmara’s assassination.

queue.fact_text: On 7 March 1951, Northwest Orient Airlines Flight 307 crashed into a residential area of Minneapolis after striking a flagpole and losing a wing on approach, killing all 13 on board and two people on the ground. The disaster highlighted how even routine flights can turn catastrophic in seconds when visibility, altitude, and urban obstacles intertwine.

episode.title: Morning Drops #40 - Diving With Orcas
episode.description: Caroline takes us into Arctic waters where orcas and humpbacks gather in winter. Gentle cinematic swells and soft rhythmic pulses mirror the cold light of the north and the fragility of underwater encounters.

queue.match_reason: Both story and episode balance awe of human/animal journeys with the quiet sense of risk inherent in moving through air or icy seas.

queue.fact_text: On 7 March 1965, around 600 civil rights marchers began a peaceful protest from Selma to Montgomery, Alabama, only to be brutally attacked by state troopers and local police on the Edmund Pettus Bridge. The televised violence of "Bloody Sunday" shocked the United States and became a turning point that helped spur passage of the Voting Rights Act.

episode.title: Morning Drops #08 - (un)Boxing
episode.description: An episode about breaking free from expectations and labels. Music that helps us unbox ourselves and embrace who we truly are.
songs: Kendrick Lamar - The Blacker the Berry

queue.match_reason: Conscious rap about identity, race, and resistance resonates powerfully with the struggle for voting rights and dignity on Bloody Sunday.

queue.fact_text: Also on 7 March 1965, Aeroflot Flight 542 broke apart in flight over the remote Yermakovsky District in Siberia, killing all 31 people aboard. The crash, attributed to structural failure in extreme winter conditions, underscored how harsh environments can quietly stress technology until it gives way.

episode.title: Morning Drops #14 - Slow Planet
episode.description: A pause: a quiet return to slower rhythms, sparked by a live set under the summer sky. A moment to breathe and settle into the week with downtempo, trip-hop, and chill electronics.

queue.match_reason: The gentle, suspended mood of Slow Planet offers a contemplative counterpoint to a silent midair breakup over frozen Siberia and the fragility of flight.

queue.fact_text: On 7 March 1986, divers from the USS Preserver located the crew cabin of the Space Shuttle Challenger on the Atlantic Ocean floor, weeks after the shuttle broke apart 73 seconds into flight. Their work, carried out in cold, murky depths, helped investigators piece together what had happened far above the clouds.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset: what if emptiness is not a void but space to create? An energising selection of feel-good tracks to launch your day.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

queue.match_reason: An episode about reframing emptiness and space connects with the emotional and literal void left by Challenger and the search beneath the ocean surface.

queue.fact_text: On 7 March 1989, diplomatic relations between Iran and the United Kingdom were severed following uproar over Salman Rushdie’s novel "The Satanic Verses" and a death sentence declared against him. A work of fiction had triggered a geopolitical rupture, showing how stories can become flashpoints between cultures and states.

episode.title: Morning Drops #38 - History of Flamenco
episode.description: A journey into the history of Flamenco, guided by Yasmin and Almudena from Madrid. Exploring the roots and evolution of a tradition that is emotionally raw and culturally rich.
songs: La Niña de los Peines - Al Gurugú
Paco de Lucía - Entre dos aguas
Camarón de la Isla - La leyenda del tiempo

queue.match_reason: An episode about a deeply expressive cultural tradition parallels a global conflict born from literature, belief, and the power of art to provoke.

queue.fact_text: On 7 March 2006, the militant group Lashkar‑e‑Taiba coordinated bomb attacks in Varanasi, India, targeting a Hindu temple and railway station in one of the world’s oldest continuously inhabited cities. The bombings aimed to inflame religious tensions in a city where sacred rituals and the Ganges have flowed together for millennia.

episode.title: Morning Drops #39 - Moving Meditation
episode.description: An episode inspired by travel as a way to see more clearly. Balearic warmth, world music, and quiet grooves accompany subtle inner shifts when we’re out of our usual rhythm.
songs: España Circo Este - Dormo poco e sogno molto
Talking Heads - This Must Be the Place (Naive Melody)

queue.match_reason: A world‑travel, reflection‑based episode offers a meditative countercurrent to violence in one of humanity’s great spiritual crossroads.

queue.fact_text: On 7 March 2007, the British House of Commons voted in favor of making the House of Lords a fully elected chamber, a radical reform proposal for an institution rooted in heredity and appointment. Although the change was never fully implemented, the vote marked a moment when centuries of tradition were openly weighed against modern democratic ideals.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Episode two of the main Sedna series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

queue.match_reason: A journey into new territories and roots reflects the tension between tradition and transformation at the heart of Lords reform.

queue.fact_text: Also on 7 March 2007, Garuda Indonesia Flight 200 overran the runway and burst into flames while landing at Yogyakarta’s Adisutjipto International Airport, killing 21 people. Several passengers survived by forcing open exits and running through burning fuel, and the crash prompted wide‑ranging safety reforms in Indonesian aviation.

episode.title: Morning Drops #48 - Cosmic Monday
episode.description: Built around Italian dance records, this episode traces how disco rhythms slowly became electronic language. A bright, futuristic feeling for mornings when you need extra energy without aggression.
songs: Giovanotti Mondani Meccanici - Don't Ask Me Why

queue.match_reason: The sleek, high‑tempo feel of electronic flight‑like disco contrasts with the abrupt failure of a landing, hinting at how technology can both lift and endanger us.

queue.fact_text: On 7 March 2009, two British soldiers were shot dead and four others wounded at Massereene Barracks in Northern Ireland, the first fatal attack on British forces there since the official end of The Troubles. The Real IRA’s ambush was a grim reminder that even after peace agreements, old conflicts can flicker back to life.

episode.title: Morning Drops #17 - Steel City
episode.description: A short trip through Sheffield's sounds — from electronic textures to raw guitars and sharp storytelling. Four tracks, one city, different edges of the same story.
songs: The Human League - The Sound Of The Crowd

queue.match_reason: Gritty UK guitar and industrial textures from another British city echo the urban tension and unresolved histories behind renewed violence in Northern Ireland.

queue.fact_text: On 7 March 2021, a series of massive explosions ripped through a military barracks complex near the city of Bata in Equatorial Guinea, killing at least 108 people and injuring hundreds. Authorities blamed poorly stored explosives ignited by a nearby fire, exposing how invisible risks can accumulate in the heart of a community.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - A Trip-Hop Journey
episode.description: A journey through the roots and evolution of trip-hop, from Bristol’s early experiments to cinematic sampling and global downtempo grooves. Exploring a genre that blurred boundaries.
songs: Tricky - Hell Is Round the Corner
DJ Shadow - Midnight in a Perfect World

queue.match_reason: Trip‑hop’s smoky, unsettling atmospheres parallel the surreal aftermath of an urban explosion and the way shock lingers in a city’s air.

queue.fact_text: On 7 March 2024, Sweden formally joined NATO as its 32nd member, ending over two centuries of military non‑alignment that had shaped its foreign policy since the Napoleonic era. The shift, driven largely by security concerns after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, marked a profound rethinking of national identity and regional balance.

episode.title: Morning Drops #10 - The One With Many Names
episode.description: Yasmin guides us through the glamorous world of Ghost, the Swedish band that turned rock into ritual. From masked clergy to shifting identities, diving into the music and myth of one of the most theatrical acts of our time.

queue.match_reason: An episode about a shape‑shifting Swedish band aligns with Sweden itself shifting its long‑held role by stepping into a new collective defense identity.

queue.fact_text: Also on this day in 2024, a New Mexico jury found armorer Hannah Gutierrez‑Reed guilty of involuntary manslaughter in the on‑set shooting death of cinematographer Halyna Hutchins during the film "Rust." It was the first time in U.S. history that a film crew member was criminally convicted for a fatality on a movie set, forcing the industry to confront the cost of realism and risk.

episode.title: Morning Drops #42 - The Joy Of Learning
episode.description: Yasmin explores learning as a lifelong process, looking at persistence, mistakes, and the emotional landscape of challenging oneself. A softer, more realistic approach to failure and growth.

queue.match_reason: An episode about learning through mistakes and reshaping systems resonates with the film industry’s reckoning over safety after the Rust verdict.

queue.fact_text: On 7 March 1902, in the Second Boer War’s Battle of Tweebosch, Koos de la Rey’s mobile Boer force captured most of a British column, including its commanding general. This humiliating defeat for a global empire at the hands of local fighters using fast horses and intimate terrain knowledge foreshadowed the asymmetrical conflicts of the 20th century.

episode.title: Morning Drops #16 - The Sound of Tuareg
episode.description: A journey into the sound of the Tuareg: from traditional chants and desert rituals to the electrified rhythms of exile and resistance.

queue.match_reason: Returning briefly to the desert motif underscores how mobile fighters rooted in harsh landscapes—from Boers to Tuareg—have repeatedly challenged great powers.

queue.fact_text: On 7 March 1989, as controversy over "The Satanic Verses" spread, the diplomatic break between Iran and the UK showed that a single novel could influence not just readers but embassies, treaties, and security details. The affair became a case study in how art, religion, and free expression collide in a globalized world.

episode.title: Morning Drops #29 - Berber France Meets Arabic Italy
episode.description: Yasmin revisits a friendship built around music, identity, and cultural crossings between Algeria, France, and Italy. Songs that exist in multiple versions echo stories of belonging and translation.

queue.match_reason: An episode about cultural hybridity and contested identity mirrors the Rushdie affair’s clash of languages, homelands, and interpretations.

queue.fact_text: Bloody Sunday in Selma didn’t succeed in stopping the marches; after 7 March 1965, activists regrouped and returned twice more to the road toward Montgomery. The perseverance behind those later, less televised steps reminds us that turning points are often made of quiet returns, not just dramatic images.

episode.title: Morning Drops #15 - Unfreezing
episode.description: A short, honest Morning Drop with no grand theme — just four tracks, a bit of voice, and the decision not to wait for the 'right' moment. Room to breathe today.
songs: Jane Birkin & Serge Gainsbourg - Je t’aime… moi non plus
Bernard Badie - In Love (DJ Quad's at Night Mix)

queue.match_reason: An episode about imperfect, everyday steps forward fits the quieter, persistent marches that followed the shock of Bloody Sunday.

queue.fact_text: From Napoleon’s battles to Sweden joining NATO, 7 March threads through history as a date where borders are challenged — on land, at sea, in the air, and even in ideas. The events on this day show how small decisions and sudden shocks can ripple outward for decades, reshaping maps, movements, and the stories we tell about ourselves.

episode.title: Sedna FM #01 - November Bomb
episode.description: The first Family & Friends radio episode from Yasmin Sarbaoui and Gaia Parolini. Where it all began — a life soundtrack that launched Sedna FM into the universe: raw, heartfelt, and full of the energy that defines everything that followed.
songs: Canserbero - De la vida como pelicula, tragedia, comedia y ficcion

queue.match_reason: A ‘where it all began’ episode is a fitting soundtrack to zooming out and seeing 7 March as a recurring starting point for new chapters in history.

published.fact_text: In 1876, Alexander Graham Bell was granted a patent for an invention he cautiously called the “telephone,” at a time when most people had never even heard a voice travel over a wire. His early experiments turned vibrations in the air into electrical signals and back again, laying the foundations for the global web of conversations that now span the planet and reach into space.

episode.title: Morning Drops #27 - Nomophobia
episode.description: Yasmin reflects on what happened when she recently lost her phone and why it felt freeing. A dive into the psychology of our relationship with smartphones: from nomophobia (the fear of being without a phone), to digital burnout and cognitive overload, to the surprising benefits of boredom and the brain's default mode network. An invitation to rethink how we connect and disconnect.

published.match_reason: An episode about our relationship with phones perfectly echoes Bell’s first step toward a world connected by voice and signals.

published.fact_text: On 7 March 1931, Finland inaugurated its Parliament House in Helsinki, a monumental building whose austere granite façade hides finely tuned acoustic spaces and carefully orchestrated natural light. The structure was designed not only as a seat of power, but as a physical manifesto of a young nation’s modern identity.

episode.title: Morning Drops #45 - It Can't Rain Forever
episode.description: Gaia returns from the vivid landscapes of Morocco to open a space for reflection about transition and constancy. The musical selection moves from orchestral depth into the golden warmth of psychedelic soul, celebrating the grounding force of friendship and continuity.

published.match_reason: A reflective, hopeful episode about continuity and change pairs with a young nation cementing its democratic home in stone.

published.fact_text: During the Second Boer War, on 7 March 1902, Boer forces under Koos de la Rey defeated a larger British column at the Battle of Tweebosch using mobility, terrain knowledge, and hit‑and‑run tactics. It was one of the last major Boer victories, showing how intimate familiarity with landscape can outplay sheer numbers.

episode.title: Morning Drops #16 - The Sound of Tuareg
episode.description: A journey into the sound of the Tuareg: from traditional chants and desert rituals to the electrified rhythms of exile and resistance. Music shaped by sand, memory, and survival.

published.match_reason: Both the Boer commandos and Tuareg music arise from deep desert and semi-arid landscapes, where survival, mobility, and resistance are shaped by the terrain.

published.fact_text: On 7 March 1814, Napoleon won the Battle of Craonne in northeastern France, tactically defeating a larger Russian and Prussian force at the cost of heavy casualties. It was a sharp, narrow victory in a wider campaign that was already slipping beyond his control, a reminder that even the most brilliant maneuvers can’t always reverse the tide.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description: Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension and shaped by conviction, remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest. From unrest to resistance, the episode traces rupture and legacy.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

published.match_reason: A ferocious, politically charged rock episode mirrors the intensity and mounting inevitability surrounding Napoleon’s late‑war battlefield victories.
